note: naked (HTML source only) vs harness (rendered page + axe + a11y tree + contrast). Renderable pages only — NOT the source-code bench tasks. Separate from leaderboard.json.
methodology: naked is given ONLY the page HTML; harness opens the rendered page. On the render-dependent suite the contrast violations live in EXTERNAL CSS — computed/rendered values a source-reading model cannot determine (cascade, overrides, background blending), so naked is blind to them while the harness measures them with check_contrast. The inline-CSS baseline keeps colors in the HTML, so naked can read them and the two modes roughly tie — which is exactly why the harness edge shows up only on render-dependent pages. Pages are authored eval fixtures with complete gold annotations + accessible decoys (so over-reporting is penalised); they are NOT scraped single-PR tasks (those have incomplete ground truth and cannot fairly measure precision).
headline: Combined (18 pages, 56 findings): harness recall 83.9% / precision 82.5% vs naked 60.7% / 69.4%. Render-dependent subset (8 pages): harness 87.5% / 87.5% vs naked 33.3% / 42.1%.
